name: Leg of Lamb w/ Rice and Onion Puree
recipeIngredient: 5 Garlic cloves; peeled, cut into 1/4-inch slivers
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Trim excess fat from the leg of lamb, leaving a thin layer.
3. Using a small knife, poke holes in the meat and stuff with garlic slivers.
4. Rub the surface of the lamb with olive oil.
5. Place onions and thyme on one side of a roasting pan.
6. Sprinkle lamb with salt and pepper, fat side down on top of onions.
7. Roast in oven for 1 hour at 350°F.
8. Turn lamb fat side up and return to oven. Roast another 20-30 minutes (or 20 minutes per pound) until a meat thermometer reads 145°F for medium rare.
9. Let the lamb rest on cutting board for 15 minutes before carving.
10. Scrape onions into food processor, add rice, cream, nutmeg, and salt; pulse until smooth puree is achieved.
When serving:
1. Transfer puree to vegetable dish.
2. Slice lamb and arrange on meat platter.
